title: US and Iran Reach Peace Deal to End Conflict
subtitle: Pakistan announces a historic agreement as both nations commit to stopping military operations on all fronts.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have officially reached a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict. Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced the deal, which includes a permanent stop to military operations. President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement and ordered an end to the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Despite recent tensions caused by Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon, both sides are moving toward a formal signing ceremony. The agreement is expected to restore global shipping routes and address concerns regarding nuclear materials.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗已正式達成和平協議，以結束雙方為期三個月的衝突。巴基斯坦總理夏巴茲·夏立夫宣布了這項協議，內容包括永久停止軍事行動。川普總統證實了該協議，並下令解除美國對荷莫茲海峽的海軍封鎖。儘管以色列在黎巴嫩的空襲造成了近期緊張局勢，但雙方正朝著正式簽署儀式邁進。預計該協議將恢復全球航運路線，並解決有關核材料的疑慮。

paragraphs.en: A major peace agreement has been reached between the United States and Iran, marking a significant turn in the three-month conflict that has shaken the Middle East. According to Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, both nations have agreed to the immediate and permanent end of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon. This announcement follows weeks of intense negotiations mediated by Pakistan, which has played a central role in bringing the two sides together.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗達成了一項重大和平協議，標誌著這場震撼中東地區、為期三個月的衝突出現了重大轉折。根據巴基斯坦總理夏巴茲·夏立夫（Shehbaz Sharif）的說法，兩國已同意立即且永久地停止包括黎巴嫩在內的所有戰線上的軍事行動。這項宣布是在巴基斯坦居中斡旋數週後發布的，巴基斯坦在促成雙方達成共識的過程中扮演了核心角色。

paragraphs.en: US President Donald Trump confirmed the deal on his social media platform, calling the agreement with the Islamic Republic of Iran "complete." As part of the new arrangement, President Trump announced that he has authorized the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. This narrow waterway is a vital route for global oil and gas supplies. By removing the US naval blockade that previously restricted movement in the area, the president signaled a return to normal shipping operations, telling the world, "Let the oil flow!"
paragraphs.zh: 美國總統川普（Donald Trump）在他的社群媒體平台上證實了這項協議，稱其與伊朗伊斯蘭共和國達成的協議是「完整的」。作為新安排的一部分，川普總統宣布他已授權開放荷莫茲海峽。這條狹窄的水道是全球石油與天然氣供應的關鍵航線。透過解除先前限制該區域航行的美國海軍封鎖，總統釋出了恢復正常航運作業的訊號，並向全世界宣告：「讓石油流動起來！」

paragraphs.en: While the agreement is a major step forward, the path to peace has been difficult. The final stages of the talks were nearly derailed by an Israeli airstrike in Beirut on Sunday. The attack destroyed a building in the southern suburbs, resulting in three deaths and several injuries. The strike caused immediate frustration in Washington and Tehran. President Trump expressed his disappointment, telling the Axios news site that the Israeli action had delayed the final signing of the deal by several hours. He also criticized the Israeli leadership for the timing of the attack, noting that it happened on a day when the two sides were very close to reaching a final agreement.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這項協議是向前邁出的重要一步，但和平之路依然艱難。談判的最後階段差點因為以色列週日在貝魯特發動的空襲而破局。這次攻擊摧毀了南部郊區的一棟建築，造成三人死亡及多人受傷。這場空襲在華盛頓與德黑蘭引發了立即的挫折感。川普總統表達了他的失望，他告訴 Axios 新聞網站，以色列的行動導致協議的最終簽署延遲了數小時。他也批評以色列領導層選擇了錯誤的攻擊時機，並指出這場攻擊發生在雙方即將達成最終協議的當天。

paragraphs.en: Iranian officials also reacted strongly to the events in Lebanon. Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, a lead negotiator for Tehran, warned that the strike showed that the United States either lacked the will or the ability to control its allies. Other military leaders in Iran stated that their forces remained on high alert, with their "finger on the trigger," ready to respond if they felt provoked. Despite these threats, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian confirmed that the country’s top security body supports the "path of dialogue," suggesting that the government is committed to the peace process.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗官員也對黎巴嫩發生的事件做出了強烈反應。德黑蘭首席談判代表穆罕默德-巴吉爾·卡利巴夫（Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf）警告稱，這次空襲顯示美國要麼缺乏意願，要麼缺乏能力來控制其盟友。伊朗其他軍事領袖表示，他們的部隊仍處於高度戒備狀態，隨時準備在感到受挑釁時做出回應。儘管有這些威脅，伊朗總統馬蘇德·佩澤希齊揚（Masoud Pezeshkian）仍證實，該國最高安全機構支持「對話之路」，這顯示政府致力於和平進程。

paragraphs.en: International leaders have welcomed the news, though they continue to urge caution. United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres strongly criticized the recent violence in Beirut, noting that the strikes occurred at a critical moment when the world was expecting a peaceful resolution. He called on all parties to show maximum restraint to ensure the deal remains successful. Meanwhile, US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth told CBS that the negotiations are on track. He emphasized that for the peace to hold, Iran must ensure that its partners, such as the militant group Hezbollah, also pull back from further attacks.
paragraphs.zh: 國際領袖對此消息表示歡迎，但仍呼籲保持謹慎。聯合國秘書長古特瑞斯（Antonio Guterres）強烈譴責貝魯特近期的暴力事件，並指出這些空襲發生在全世界期待和平解決方案的關鍵時刻。他呼籲各方展現最大程度的克制，以確保協議能夠成功。與此同時，美國國防部長皮特·赫格塞斯（Pete Hegseth）告訴哥倫比亞廣播公司（CBS），談判進展順利。他強調，為了維持和平，伊朗必須確保其夥伴（例如武裝團體真主黨）也停止進一步的攻擊。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the focus has shifted to the formalization of the agreement. Prime Minister Sharif announced that a signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday, June 19, in Switzerland. While the exact details of the entire document are not yet public, the deal is expected to cover several key areas. Beyond ending the fighting, President Trump has indicated that the agreement includes plans to eliminate Iran’s stockpile of enriched uranium, which is a material that can be used to create nuclear weapons. The president stated that the US would work to remove this "nuclear dust" to ensure future security.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，焦點已轉向協議的正式化。夏立夫總理宣布，簽署儀式定於 6 月 19 日星期五在瑞士舉行。雖然整份文件的確切細節尚未公開，但預計該協議將涵蓋幾個關鍵領域。除了結束戰鬥外，川普總統表示，協議內容包括消除伊朗的濃縮鈾庫存，這是一種可用於製造核武器的材料。總統聲明，美國將致力於清除這些「核塵埃」，以確保未來的安全。

paragraphs.en: Experts remain hopeful but realistic about the challenges that lie ahead. HA Hellyer, a regional expert at the Royal United Services Institute in London, noted that while the deal is a positive development, there is still a lack of trust between the two countries. He pointed out that both sides have internal groups that may not want the deal to succeed. However, with the Strait of Hormuz set to reopen and military operations coming to a halt, the international community is watching closely to see if this historic agreement can bring lasting stability to the region.
paragraphs.zh: 專家們對未來的挑戰保持希望，但也持務實態度。倫敦皇家聯合軍事研究所（Royal United Services Institute）的區域專家 H.A. 海利爾（H.A. Hellyer）指出，雖然這項協議是一個積極的發展，但兩國之間仍缺乏信任。他指出，雙方內部都有可能不希望協議成功的團體。然而，隨著荷莫茲海峽即將重新開放且軍事行動即將停止，國際社會正密切關注這項歷史性協議能否為該地區帶來持久的穩定。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prevent a process or plan from continuing as intended.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使（計畫、進程）脫軌、受阻或失敗。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指火車出軌，這裡引申為計畫受阻。文中：The final stages of the talks were nearly derailed by an Israeli airstrike in Beirut on Sunday.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Making progress and likely to achieve the desired result.
keyTerms.noteZh: 形容事情正朝著正確的方向發展。文中：Meanwhile, US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth told CBS that the negotiations are on track.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To withdraw or retreat from an activity or position.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 撤退、後撤、停止（某種行動）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指軍事或武裝行動上的退縮。文中：He emphasized that for the peace to hold, Iran must ensure that its partners, such as the militant group Hezbollah, also pull back from further attacks.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Peace deal between US and Iran, Pakistan says, with strait of Hormuz to re-open

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Iran war: Pakistan announces US-Iran peace deal reached

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Trump condemns Israeli strike in Beirut, warning attacks threaten deal on U.S-Iran war

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Near Potential Peace Agreement After Weeks of Conflict
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump claims a peace deal with Iran is close to being signed. The agreement aims to stop Iran from building nuclear weapons and reopen the important Strait of Hormuz. While some international leaders are optimistic about a quick resolution, Iranian officials have asked for more patience. Recent days have seen both threats of military action and signs of diplomatic progress. The situation remains uncertain as both sides try to frame the potential agreement as a victory.

previousArticles.title: Trump Cancels Planned Strikes on Iran, Claims Peace Deal is Near
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump has cancelled planned military strikes against Iran, citing progress in peace negotiations. He claims a final agreement could be signed as early as this weekend in Europe. However, Iranian officials have denied that a final decision has been reached, stating that they will not compromise on their 'red lines.' Meanwhile, the U.S. naval blockade of Iran remains in effect until a deal is finalized. The international community continues to watch the situation closely as oil prices fluctuate in response to the news.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Move Closer to Potential Peace Agreement
previousArticles.summaryEn: President Donald Trump claims a peace deal with Iran could be signed as early as Sunday. The agreement aims to stop Iran from developing nuclear weapons and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz. While some international leaders are optimistic, Iranian officials have suggested that the timeline is not yet certain. Both sides are currently trying to present the potential agreement as a victory for their own country. The situation remains tense as both nations continue to balance diplomatic talks with military actions.

quiz.question: What specific event nearly prevented the finalization of the peace agreement?

options.A: A disagreement between US and Iranian negotiators regarding the Strait of Hormuz.
options.B: An Israeli military operation conducted in the southern suburbs of Beirut.
options.C: A failure by the Iranian government to secure support from its top security body.
options.D: A public warning issued by Iranian military leaders about their readiness to fight.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，以色列在貝魯特發動的空襲（Israeli airstrike）幾乎讓談判脫軌（nearly derailed），並導致協議簽署延遲了數小時。選項 A 錯誤，因為荷姆茲海峽的開放是協議的一部分，而非導致談判中斷的原因。選項 C 錯誤，文中提到伊朗安全機構其實支持對話。選項 D 雖然是伊朗軍方的反應，但那是對空襲的回應，並非導致談判險些破局的直接原因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between the US and its allies during this negotiation process?

options.A: The US has full control over the military decisions made by all its regional partners.
options.B: The US and its allies are in complete agreement regarding the terms of the Iran deal.
options.C: The US faces challenges in ensuring that its allies' actions do not undermine its diplomatic efforts.
options.D: The US intends to cut all military ties with Israel as a result of the recent airstrike.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。從第三段川普對以色列空襲的批評，以及第四段伊朗談判代表指出美國似乎無法控制其盟友（lacked the will or the ability to control its allies）的言論，可以推論出美國在外交斡旋時，難以完全約束盟國的軍事行動，這對其外交努力造成了挑戰。選項 A 與文意衝突，因為美國顯然無法控制以色列。選項 B 無法由文中得知，且川普對以色列的批評顯示雙方立場並不一致。選項 D 過度推論，文中並未提到美國要斷絕與以色列的軍事關係。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the author mean by the term "nuclear dust"?

options.A: The physical debris left behind after a nuclear explosion.
options.B: The stockpile of enriched uranium that could be used for weapons.
options.C: Radioactive particles that have contaminated the environment in Iran.
options.D: The metaphorical remnants of failed past diplomatic negotiations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第六段上下文，總統提到協議包含消除伊朗的「濃縮鈾庫存」（stockpile of enriched uranium），並緊接著稱其為「nuclear dust」。這裡的 dust 是比喻用法，指代這些危險的核原料，而非字面上的灰塵。選項 A 與 C 均為字面上的誤解，忽略了文中對濃縮鈾的明確定義。選項 D 雖然是隱喻，但與文中提到的具體軍備控制目標不符。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the current status of the US-Iran situation?

options.A: A lasting peace has been fully established and all regional tensions have been resolved.
options.B: The agreement is finalized and has already led to the complete disarmament of all regional groups.
options.C: Despite a promising peace deal, the process remains fragile due to lingering mistrust and regional volatility.
options.D: The peace agreement is expected to fail because of the intense opposition from the United Nations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體論述了雙方雖然達成了歷史性的和平協議，但仍面臨空襲干擾、缺乏信任以及內部反對勢力等挑戰。專家與官員的態度皆顯示出「審慎樂觀」的立場。選項 A 過於樂觀，忽視了文中提到的困難與不信任。選項 B 錯誤，因為協議尚未正式簽署，且並未達成全面解除武裝。選項 D 錯誤，聯合國秘書長是支持並呼籲各方克制的，並未反對該協議。
